agency: New York City Law Department (Office of the Corporation Counsel)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/law (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace) — the same shared citywide chassis, with no Law-specific portal or application system exposed. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Law Department (LAW)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 7 assets with column schemas. Separately confirmed that the claims-and-settlements ledger is published NOT by Law but by the Office of the Comptroller (dataset ex6k-ym48).
headline_findings: The Law Department is an AGENCY-FACING legal office — the City's in-house law firm — with almost no citizen-facing transactions. Its public site runs the same shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' chassis (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) as every other agency; there is no Law-specific portal or application system.

Its Open Data footprint is thin and stale: only 7 datasets under 'Law Department (LAW)', every one flagged 'No' automation and 'Annually' updated — a civil-litigation case index, a list of legal divisions, three parallel publication feeds (press releases / speeches / columns), M/WBE contracting statistics, and a pro bono Public Service Program firm list.

The data that matters most is not the Law Department's to publish: the ledger of claims filed against the City and settlement dollars paid lives with the Office of the Comptroller (ex6k-ym48). The Law Department's own litigation dataset (pjgc-h7uv) is a case INDEX, not the citywide claims record.
There is no service layer and no write surface at all — no API, no OpenAPI, no JSON beyond the SODA endpoints on the seven annual datasets.

Net-new write surface: submit a legal internship / fellowship application (LawInternshipApplication) — the single transaction a member of the public actually initiates with this agency, today handled only via email/PDF instructions on the Careers page. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 7 Law Department datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The only real, open Law API — thin, annual, manually-updated reference data.
content only — About, Divisions, Careers, Public Resources, News. Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API, no application system.
Claims Report — Underlying Settlements and Claims Filed. The authoritative claims/settlement ledger for City litigation; published by the Comptroller, NOT the Law Department.

fruit.name: Civil Litigation Case Index
opendata_match: Case-Related Information About Civil Litigation pjgc-h7uv (17c)
fruit.notes: The Law Department's own index of civil-litigation matters — matter name, docket/index, court, judge, handling division, disposition, dates, Lead BBL, and payout/received/expense totals. Keyed on docket/index. NOTE: the authoritative claims/settlement ledger is the Comptroller's (ex6k-ym48), not this dataset.

fruit.name: Legal Divisions
fruit.notes: The practice divisions of the Department (division name + parent department). Cross-references the 'Legal Divisions' page on nyc.gov/site/law.

fruit.name: Publications (Press Releases / Speeches / Columns)
fruit.notes: Three parallel datasets sharing one shape (title, date, linked PDF). Unified into a single Publication resource with a publicationType discriminator.

fruit.name: M/WBE Participation Statistics
opendata_match: Minority and Women-Owned Business Enterprise Statistics svyi-maaj (7c)
fruit.notes: The Department's M/WBE contracting shares by category across prime/sub contract families. Most-viewed of the seven Law datasets. Percentages stored as reported strings.

fruit.name: Public Service Program (pro bono)
fruit.notes: Private law firms donating pro bono legal services to the City since 2007. A single-column list.

fruit.name: Claims & Settlements (Comptroller, not Law)
opendata_match: Claims Report - Underlying Settlements and Claims Filed ex6k-ym48 (Office of the Comptroller)
fruit.notes: The data users most often want from 'the City's lawyers' — claims filed and settlement dollars — is published by the COMPTROLLER, not the Law Department. Documented here so the crosswalk routes claims questions to the right agency.

fruit.name: Apply for a legal internship / fellowship
fruit.notes: The single transaction a member of the public actually initiates with the Law Department. No API and no Open Data twin — the Careers page directs applicants to email/PDF instructions. The net-new WRITE surface for this agency-facing domain.

meta.platform: NYC.gov shared 'Livesite' CMS (Akamai + nginx + Dynatrace) — no Law-specific platform, portal, or application system
meta.netnew: LawInternshipApplication — submit a legal internship / fellowship application (the one citizen-initiated transaction with this agency)
meta.tagline: The City's in-house law firm publishes seven stale annual snapshots — and the claims dollars that matter belong to the Comptroller.
meta.headline: An agency-facing legal office with almost no citizen transactions: thin, annual Open Data, no service layer, and the one thing worth exposing is where the public applies to work there.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; nothing Law-specific to point at.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content or site API of any kind.
scorecard_notes.openData: 7 datasets, all 'No' automation / annual — present but thin and stale.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Only a case index is Law's; the core claims/settlement ledger belongs to the Comptroller.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write surface at all; applications handled by email/PDF.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Data on Socrata, litigation money data owned by another agency, site on a shared chassis.
